CONTENT.Honours: Italy U17UEFA European Under-17 Championship runner-up: 2013
CONTENT.Club career: Bonazzoli played at youth level for Ghedi and Montechiari, before joining Inter Milan at the age of seven.On 18 May 2014, Bonazzoli made his senior debut against Chievo Verona as a substitute for Rubén Botta in the 85th minute of a 1–2 away defeat on the last day of the Serie A season. He became the second youngest footballer to play in Serie A for Inter aged 16 years, 11 months and 27 days, 17 days older than Massimo Pellegrini, who debuted in 1982. On 6 November 2014 he made his UEFA Europa League debut in a 1–1 away draw against Saint-Étienne in the group stage, he was replaced by Joel Obi in the 66th minute.On 2 February 2015, he signed for fellow league team Sampdoria for €4.5 million, but would remain at Inter on loan until the end of the season.On 21 January 2016, he was signed by Serie B club Lanciano on a season-long loan deal, with an option to purchase. On 22 July, he joined Brescia on loan.On 5 October 2020, Bonazzoli joined Torino on loan.On 3 August 2021, US Salernitana announced the signing of Bonazzoli on a season-long loan with an option to buy. He scored two goals in his Coppa Italia debut win over Reggina.On 26 July 2022, Bonazzoli signed permanently with Salernitana on a four-year contract, for a reported fee of €5 millions.
CONTENT.Style of play: Bonazzoli models himself on former Inter forward Zlatan Ibrahimović. He is highly rated by Inter fans as a Christian Vieri-like striker, with a great physique and technical abilities.
CONTENT.International career: With the Italy U-17 side he took part at the 2013 UEFA European Under-17 Championship.He made his debut with the Italy U-21 team on 17 November 2014, in a home friendly match loss 1–0 against Denmark, becoming the youngest player to make his debut with the team at the time.

SUMMARY: Federico Bonazzoli (born 21 May 1997) is an Italian professional footballer who plays as a forward for Serie A club Hellas Verona, on loan from Salernitana.
